commonCore.title: Delivery fundamentals
commonCore.description: Relate source, review, build, verification, artifact, and release state.

commonCore.title: CLI, APIs, and structured data
commonCore.description: Use command output and provider-shaped APIs without parsing unstable human text.

commonCore.title: Authentication and authorization
commonCore.description: Separate identity establishment from permission evaluation.

commonCore.title: OAuth and OIDC
commonCore.description: Explain clients, subjects, scopes, claims, audiences, signing, and token exchange boundaries.

commonCore.title: Policy and scope
commonCore.description: Trace exact grants, denies, tenants, actions, resources, and classifications.

commonCore.title: Control and data planes
commonCore.description: Distinguish configuration authority from protected resource access.

commonCore.title: CI identity
commonCore.description: Understand least-privilege workflow authority and artifact lineage.

commonCore.title: Secrets
commonCore.description: Keep credentials synthetic, explicitly selected, bounded, and absent from evidence.

commonCore.title: Evidence
commonCore.description: Connect claims to current state, decisions, outcomes, reports, and compatibility records.

commonCore.title: Deterministic verification
commonCore.description: Use invariant or evidence evaluation rather than subjective completion claims.

commonCore.title: Cleanup and ownership
commonCore.description: Stop only owned resources and confirm terminal state after success or failure.

lessons.title: See a provider-neutral trust path
outcomes: Explain a directed principal-to-resource trust path.
Distinguish canonical graph evidence from provider parity.

hints.text: Read the path as principal, group, workload identity, and resource.
Use edge labels to explain why each traversal exists.
Run the manual graph path command from the no-Docker guide.

lessons.productionContext: Identity investigations begin by making implicit authority visible.
reflection: Which edge granted the final resource access?
What behavior did the walking skeleton not emulate?

lessons.title: Remediate Google Workspace sharing
outcomes: Trace direct and group-derived Drive access.
Remove an excessive permission while preserving approved access.

hints.text: Compare the contractor permission with the approved group path.
Inspect the exact file permission identifiers before deleting anything.
Remove only permission_contractor and rerun verification.

lessons.productionContext: Workspace sharing investigations must separate direct grants from group-derived access.
reflection: Which real Google Workspace semantics are outside this facade?

lessons.title: Narrow Microsoft delegated consent
outcomes: Revoke an excessive grant without removing intended administrative access.

hints.text: Identify the grant attached to the ordinary analyst.
Compare its scope with the administrator path.
Delete only the documented excessive OAuth2 permission grant.

lessons.productionContext: Consent review requires principal, application, scope, and tenant context rather than permission names alone.
reflection: How did delegated consent differ from group membership?
Why is this not an Azure RBAC lab?

lessons.title: Inspect a local OIDC trust flow
outcomes: Exchange a synthetic code and validate audience, claims, signature, and key rotation.

hints.text: Confirm the discovery issuer exactly matches the token issuer.
Use the JWKS key ID selected by the token header.
Rotate signing material and prove old assumptions do not silently survive.

lessons.productionContext: Workload federation depends on exact issuer, audience, signature, claim, and lifetime validation.
reflection: Which field binds a token to its intended service?
Why is the local issuer not production OIDC?

lessons.title: Constrain AWS cross-account trust
outcomes: Trace role trust and S3 authorization across accounts.
Narrow trust while preserving the intended principal path.

hints.text: Separate who can assume the role from what the role can do.
Inspect the trust policy before changing the S3 permissions policy.
Restrict the role trust to the documented intended principal and verify both invariants.

lessons.productionContext: Cross-account access combines role trust, session identity, resource authorization, and emulator limitations.
reflection: Which policy controlled assumption and which controlled S3 access?
Which Floci authorization gaps remain documented?

lessons.title: Remediate the flagship identity chain
outcomes: Trace Google to Microsoft to local OIDC to AWS authority.
Close only the contractor path while preserving administrator access.

hints.text: Find the first live provider edge unique to the risky path.
Compare the two Microsoft app-role assignments.

lessons.productionContext: Cross-provider incidents require current state from every authority plane rather than one vendor console.
reflection: Why was changing the AWS role a broader remediation?
Which intended path proved the change was not destructive?

lessons.title: Interpret release and CI evidence
outcomes: Distinguish checksums, SBOMs, provenance, smoke tests, scans, and human review.
Validate the release packager and linked-module inventory from source.

hints.text: Ask which evidence proves bytes, origin, contents, execution, or review.
Compare the generated linked modules with the committed notice inventory.
Run the release package tests before interpreting workflow claims.

lessons.productionContext: Artifact trust is a chain of complementary signals rather than a single scan result.
reflection: What do provenance and an SBOM still not prove?

lessons.title: Evaluate a governed external agent
outcomes: Validate scenario-bound agent and tool registrations.
Explain authorization, protected output, execution outcome, state capture, and replay limitations.

hints.text: Validate registrations before launching either process.
Treat the manifest as an authority ceiling and policy as a separate decision.
Replay trial:external-starter and compare every numerator with the expected report.

lessons.productionContext: Agent governance requires bounded identity, tools, policy, evidence, and honest isolation claims.
reflection: Why did process completion not produce scenario task success?

lessons.title: Author a safe data-only scenario
outcomes: Separate learner briefing from protected evaluation rules.
Adapt stable IDs, graph relationships, invariants, compatibility claims, and cleanup expectations without adding executable capabilities.

hints.text: Start with the starter metadata and learner-visible briefing.
Trace every graph reference to a declared tenant, principal, or resource.
Validate the copied starter through the public command before starting it.

lessons.productionContext: Community extensibility begins with constrained data contracts; executable integrations require a separate trust decision.
reflection: Which fields are learner-visible and which protect evaluation?
Why can a scenario not select a shell command?

paths.title: Identity and Agent Foundations
paths.audience: Self-directed cloud, IAM, security, DevOps, and agent engineers who want an evidence-driven local enterprise path.
paths.description: Progress from a no-Docker trust graph through provider-specific authorization, cross-provider remediation, release integrity, governed agents, and safe authoring orientation.
